steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context question. The blank fills the verb describing what you do to other scholars' references. The passage immediately clarifies that "a count of other scholars' citations shows" the economist is referenced frequently, so the blank must mean to count or list systematically.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘abridge’: means to shorten or condense. Shortening citations does not evaluate a scholar's importance. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘transpose’: means to rearrange or shift order. Rearranging references reveals nothing about a scholar's significance. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘enumerate’: means to count or list one by one — a precise match for "a count of other scholars' citations shows". ✓
  • Choice D — ‘circumvent’: means to get around or bypass. Bypassing citations is the opposite of using them to measure importance. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
